topic.what: Eight novel transport-layer attacks on ISO 15765-2 CAN transport protocol inducing denial of diagnostic services
topic.how: Exploiting frame characteristics (SF/FF/CF/FC), transmission mechanisms, and error handling defined in ISO 15765-2
topic.why_matters: Prevents proper vehicle diagnosis, conceals fault conditions, manipulates sensor readings, deceives technicians, impacts safety-critical emissions testing and ECU maintenance

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of attacks successfully inducing abnormal diagnostic results
characteristics.evidence: 3 of 8 attacks (A4, A5, A6) successful on 2021 Hyundai Elantra CN7
characteristics.context: 3 successful attacks from 8 total attack scenarios across multiple diagnostic functions

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time delay in ECU identifier retrieval under attack
characteristics.evidence: Attack increased diagnostic time up to 6x normal duration
characteristics.context: A4-A6 attacks causing session termination and data corruption

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ability to manipulate Diagnostic Trouble Code query results
characteristics.evidence: G-Scan3 displayed 0 DTCs (default) instead of actual faults
characteristics.context: Conceals internal errors during diagnostic reporting

characteristics.what_it_measures: Manipulation of evaporative leak test results
characteristics.evidence: Tool displayed gas leak detected without triggering error code
characteristics.context: Deceives in-vehicle systems, impacts regulatory compliance

characteristics.what_it_measures: Interference with ECU variant coding operations
characteristics.evidence: Coding request data transmission disrupted, functions inoperable
characteristics.context: Hinders component replacement and function activation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Forced termination of active diagnostic reception sessions
characteristics.evidence: A6 session override caused data spraying and overwriting of valid results
characteristics.context: A6 exploit overwrites diagnostic data with initialization values

requires: CAN bus access (OBD-II port or compromised ECU)
two distinct diagnostic tools for validation

novelty: First systematic derivation of 8 transport-layer attacks from ISO 15765-2 standard clauses
Experimental validation on real 2021 Hyundai Elantra with OEM and aftermarket tools
Identified gaps in AUTOSAR SecOC and ISO/SAE 21434 for transport-layer protection
Proposed per-attack mitigation strategies addressing protocol design flaws
open_problems: CAN-FD protocol vulnerability assessment for same attack patterns
Impact on ECU flashing and firmware update processes
Validation in broader vehicle control contexts beyond diagnostics
